skill.name: perplexity-search
skill.description: Run current web and research searches through Perplexity models using LiteLLM and OpenRouter. The skill includes a CLI search script, setup helper, model guidance, and search strategy references.
skill.summary: Search current web sources with Perplexity models through OpenRouter.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and credential patterns. Most command hits are Markdown usage examples, but the scripts do perform real outbound OpenRouter requests, read OPENROUTER_API_KEY, write result files, and can save API keys to .env files. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is publishable with warnings.

medium_findings.title: External API Calls Send User Queries to OpenRouter
medium_findings.description: The search script sends the user query to LiteLLM completion using an OpenRouter Perplexity model. This is core functionality, but it means prompts and research questions leave the local environment and are processed by an external provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The runtime code directly calls completion with the user-provided query. This is expected for the skill, but it is a real data disclosure boundary.

medium_findings.title: API Key Read and Local Secret File Creation
medium_findings.description: The scripts read OPENROUTER_API_KEY from the environment, and the setup helper can write the supplied key into an env file. This is legitimate credential configuration, but it creates local secret exposure risk if files are committed or permissions are weak.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code and documentation explicitly handle an API key and .env files. The behavior is intentional, but sensitive by design.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output and Environment File Paths
medium_findings.description: The CLI writes search results to the path passed with --output, and setup_env.py accepts a custom --env-file path. This is useful, but it can overwrite files selected by the user and should be run only in trusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are supplied through CLI arguments and written without path restrictions. This is not malicious, but it is a meaningful filesystem risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Trigger Static Command Alerts
low_findings.description: Many external command findings occur inside documentation examples that show bash, PowerShell, jq, uv, and python commands. These examples are not executed automatically by the skill, so they are mostly false positives for runtime command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced documentation examples rather than executable code paths. Users may copy them manually, but the skill does not invoke them automatically.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Provider URLs Are Expected Configuration References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to OpenRouter, LiteLLM, and Perplexity documentation or account pages. These URLs match the stated purpose of the skill and do not indicate hidden exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains are consistent with the documented OpenRouter and Perplexity integration. No unrelated or suspicious endpoint was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Plus Network Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads an API key and makes external model requests. This combination is expected for an OpenRouter client, but it should be disclosed because credentials and prompts are involved.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The static dangerous-combination heuristic is semantically real, but it aligns with the declared search-client purpose rather than showing malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Printed in Suggested Export Command
dangerous_patterns.description: After setup, setup_env.py prints an export command containing the API key value. This can expose the key in terminal logs or shared support output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code formats args.api_key directly into terminal output. The exposure is local, but the pattern is clear.

content.value_statement: Current research and technical facts change faster than model training data. This skill adds Perplexity search through OpenRouter so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can retrieve grounded answers with citations.
actual_capabilities: Runs Perplexity searches through LiteLLM and OpenRouter from a Python CLI.
Supports Sonar, Sonar Pro, Sonar Pro Search, and reasoning model choices.
Checks whether LiteLLM and OPENROUTER_API_KEY are configured before use.
Prints answers and usage statistics, with optional JSON result saving.
Includes setup, model selection, troubleshooting, and query strategy documentation.
limitations: Requires a paid or credited OpenRouter account and a valid API key.
Sends user queries to external model providers through OpenRouter.
Citation availability depends on the selected model and provider response.
The setup helper stores secrets locally if users choose .env file setup.

use_cases.title: Review Recent Literature
use_cases.description: Find current papers, clinical results, and scientific summaries after a model knowledge cutoff.

use_cases.title: Verify Technical Decisions
use_cases.description: Compare current tools, frameworks, and practices with source-backed answers before implementation.

use_cases.title: Track Market or Policy Updates
use_cases.description: Search recent announcements, regulations, or product changes with a reusable CLI workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Current Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Perplexity Search to answer: What are the latest developments in [topic]? Include source citations and publication dates when available.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Literature Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Find recent scientific evidence in a defined area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for peer-reviewed research from [date range] about [topic]. Summarize the strongest findings, key limitations, and cited sources.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Current Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate tools, models, or methods with current sources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the most appropriate Perplexity model to compare [option A] and [option B] for [use case]. Include recent benchmarks, risks, and citations.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Step Research
prompt_templates.scenario: Use deeper search for complex analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run an advanced Perplexity search on [research question]. Break the answer into evidence, disagreements, source quality, open questions, and recommended follow-up searches.

output_examples.input: Search recent CAR-T therapy clinical trial results for B-cell lymphoma.
output_examples.output: A concise evidence summary with trial names, reported outcomes, adverse events, dates, and links or citations returned by the provider.

output_examples.input: Compare PyTorch and TensorFlow for transformer model development in 2026.
output_examples.output: A comparison covering ecosystem support, performance evidence, deployment tradeoffs, and source-backed recommendations.

output_examples.input: Find current guidance on OpenRouter Perplexity model selection.
output_examples.output: A model selection note explaining when to use Sonar, Sonar Pro, Sonar Pro Search, and reasoning variants.

best_practices: Use environment variables or protected secret storage for OPENROUTER_API_KEY.
Include date ranges, source preferences, and domain terms in each search query.
Set token limits and choose lower-cost models for simple fact checks.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private customer data, secrets, or unreleased research into external searches.
Do not commit .env files or generated outputs that contain sensitive queries.
Do not use the most expensive model for simple lookup tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Perplexity API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses Perplexity models through OpenRouter, so an OpenRouter API key is required.

faq.question: Does it send my queries outside my computer?
faq.answer: Yes. Queries are sent through LiteLLM to OpenRouter and the selected Perplexity model provider.

faq.question: Can it save search results?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI can save results to a user-specified JSON file with the --output option.

faq.question: Which model should I use first?
faq.answer: Start with Sonar Pro for general research. Use Sonar Pro Search for deeper multi-step questions.

faq.question: Is the setup script safe to use?
faq.answer: It is useful, but it writes API keys to a local env file if requested. Protect that file and exclude it from version control.

faq.question: Does the skill guarantee citations?
faq.answer: No. It attempts to return grounded answers, but citation detail depends on the model and provider response.
